blueprint.name: Smart Home Battery
blueprint.description: Generic Home Assistant blueprint for up to four batteries. It allocates discharge from house demand, can trigger opportunistic charging during real export, and drives each battery only through custom actions.
message.invalid_sensor: House power sensor {{ house_power_sensor }} is unavailable or not numeric.
validation.no_actions.suffix: is enabled by its state of charge sensor, but no Set discharge actions or Set charge actions are configured. Add at least one of these actions, or clear the state of charge sensor to disable this battery slot.
validation.no_power.suffix: has actions configured, but both maximum power limits are set to 0 W. Set a non-zero Maximum discharge power or Maximum charge power to make this slot usable.
validation.no_matching_interface.suffix: has partial settings, but no usable control direction is complete. At least one direction needs both a non-zero maximum power and the matching Set action.
input.house_power_sensor.name: House power sensor
input.house_power_sensor.description: Main house power sensor. The blueprint expects positive values for import and negative values for export.
input.blocking_entities.name: Blocking entities
input.blocking_entities.description: Optional entities that must all stay off for the blueprint to run. If any selected entity is on, unknown, or unavailable, the blueprint forces every managed battery back to neutral and stops all custom charge and discharge actions.
input.battery.soc_sensor.name: State of charge sensor
input.battery.soc_sensor.description: Leave empty to ignore this battery slot. Otherwise, this selector only shows battery sensors that report a charge percentage, and the chosen sensor enables the slot. An enabled slot must also expose at least one usable direction: a non-zero maximum power and the matching Set action.
input.battery.max_discharge_w.name: Maximum discharge power
input.battery.max_discharge_w.descriptio: Manual discharge limit used by the allocator for this battery.
input.battery.max_charge_w.name: Maximum charge power
input.battery.max_charge_w.description: Manual charge limit used by the allocator for opportunistic charging. Set to 0 if this battery must never be charged by the blueprint.
input.battery.priority_discharge.name: Priority on discharge
input.battery.priority_discharge.descrip: Prioritized batteries are discharged first, ordered by their state of charge. Non-priority batteries come afterwards, also ordered by state of charge.
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.name: Command cooldown
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.descripti: Minimum delay between two active set actions for this battery. Set to 0 to disable it. Stop and neutralization actions can still run immediately for safety.
input.battery.set_discharge_actions.name: Set discharge actions
input.battery.set_discharge_actions.desc: Required when Maximum discharge power is greater than 0 W. These custom actions are executed when this battery should actively discharge. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_discharge_w, house_power_w, import_need_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions. For integrations like Zendure, this can write a signed helper entity that the vendor integration consumes.
input.battery.stop_discharge_actions.des: Optional custom actions executed when discharge must stop. Configure them as idempotent if possible, especially for batteries controlled only through actions.
input.battery.set_charge_actions.name: Set charge actions
input.battery.set_charge_actions.descrip: Required when Maximum charge power is greater than 0 W. These custom actions are executed when this battery should actively charge from surplus export. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_charge_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions. For integrations like Zendure, this can write a negative signed helper value.
input.battery.stop_charge_actions.name: Stop charge actions
input.battery.stop_charge_actions.descri: Optional custom actions executed when charging must stop. Configure them as idempotent if possible, especially for batteries controlled only through actions.
